name: RJC Charter Bus Svc LLC
recent_news: USDOT 2144862 authority became inactive June 23, 2011; revoked May 22, 2017 after multiple involuntary revocations 2012-2016. Only 1 power unit / 1 driver, 11,000 miles in 2016. Effectively defunct.
fit_notes: Not a viable tuck-in. Single-vehicle motor coach operator whose FMCSA authority was revoked in 2017. Website listed in source ('Academybuscharter.com') belongs to unrelated Academy Bus LLC (1,100 buses, East Coast). No school-bus contract footprint detected. PASS.

name: Jay Medicar Transportation
recent_news: FMCSA records (USDOT 947910, MC406756) show ~50 power units / 50 drivers under predecessor Jay Medi-Car Inc; entity authority has been inactive since 2001 but business continues operating as Jay Medicar Transportation LLC on West Lake St, Chicago. Listed in Shirley Ryan AbilityLab / DSCC special-needs transportation directories.
fit_notes: Adjacent rather than core: NEMT/medicar operator with stated special-needs and 'specialized transportation for children' offering, ~100 employees, ~$7.6M revenue. Could be a SPED-route bolt-on in the Chicago/CPS market where Summit competes with First Student and Cook-Illinois, but core revenue is Medicaid NEMT, not yellow-bus contracts. Lower priority tuck-in unless Summit wants to expand into CPS SPED/NEMT routes.

name: Northwest Transportation
recent_news: Northwest Transportation Service Inc. and Valley Transportation Corp. operate as affiliated Legare-family entities at 664 Front St, Woonsocket; combined ~40+ years of school bus operations in northern RI. Valley is also affiliated with Legare Realty LLC and Standard Rent-A-Car Inc. No M&A activity identified.
fit_notes: Solid tuck-in candidate: independent Legare-family RI operator at ~46 years, ~100 employees, school+charter+SPED mix serving private/charter schools (Mt. St. Charles, RISE Prep). Long tenure of founder/president (William Legare) suggests credible succession-driven exit opportunity. Summit is light in RI; Northwest/Valley could anchor a Providence-Woonsocket corridor presence. Need to clarify whether the school-bus operations sit in Northwest Transportation Service Inc. or Valley Transportation Corp.

name: Non Emergency Transportation
districts_or_customers: 10 county franchises in central/eastern/southeastern NC + Georgia
recent_news: MedEx Medical Transport operates an in-house, NCOEMS-accredited EMS training institute with VR lab and ambulance simulator; serves as primary contractor for NC Disaster Response and exclusive provider to several major hospital systems. Locally operated, ~400 employees, 100+ vehicles.
fit_notes: NOT a school-bus operator. MedEx is an EMS/NEMT/911 provider with hospital and county franchise contracts. Out of scope for Summit's yellow-bus tuck-in thesis. PASS.

name: Jay's Transport 24/7
districts_or_customers: Washtenaw County (Ann Arbor area) Medicaid / no-fault auto / disability riders
recent_news: Small Ann Arbor-area non-emergency medical transportation operator at PO Box 130526; phone (734) 434-8005. Listed alongside Hour Transportation, MedSync, RK Medride and other SE-Michigan NEMT providers serving Washtenaw ISD-adjacent populations. No school district contracts identified; no M&A activity.
fit_notes: Sub-scale NEMT/wheelchair operator (~$1.3M revenue, ~20 employees). Not a school-bus contractor. Too small and out of Summit's primary thesis. PASS unless Summit pursues a Michigan SPED/NEMT roll-up.
